← the Atlas

Sleeping terminals — a terminal state, not a tile

Features· seedling ·proposed ·JUN 20, 2026

A plan of record for Sleep/Wake, re-grounded a second time. The first re-plan built a first-class Tile registry (PR 1435, merged) to host sleeping as a tile content kind; building on it (PR 1441) surfaced that the Tile was the wrong level — sleeping is a STATE of a terminal, and the field partition already expresses it. So we collapse the tile layer, model Terminal = active | sleeping, and let sleeping, persistence, and session-restore unify on one record with a state flag.

Contents

  • The pivot — sleeping is a terminal state
  • Before → after
  • The surgical collapse
  • The phases
  • Payoff & trade-offs

You asked for Sleep: leave a Claude Code terminal blocked for days, sleep it (its PTY, xterm, WebGL context, and agent all released — gone, like closing it), and wake it later in place with the agent resumed.

This is the plan’s second re-grounding, and it reverses the first. The first re-plan diagnosed the blocker as “there is no first-class tile in kolu — a tile is a live terminal”, and fixed it by building a Tile registry ( #1435 , merged) with a TileContent union, intending sleeping to be a second content kind. Building sleeping on it ( #1441 ) surfaced the deeper truth: sleeping is a state of a terminal, not a kind of canvas content — and the tile layer we added to host that “second kind” hosts a phantom, because the kind never materialized. So we close #1441 , collapse the tile layer, and move the discriminant where it belongs: onto the terminal.

The pivot — sleeping is a terminal state

A first-class tile would earn its keep the day the canvas holds something that isn’t a terminal (a sticky note, a web view). It doesn’t, and it isn’t on the near roadmap — so a content-kind layer above the terminal is speculative generality: a one-armed union whose only “second kind” is a mis-levelled state. Both the data-loss bug and the “wrong store” bug class on #1441 trace to that mis-levelling and to its enabling alias, TileId = TerminalId.

The clean model puts the sum on the terminal itself — and the fold already exists in the schema. surface.ts splits a terminal’s fields into a persisted base (cwd · git · intent · theme · canvasLayout — survives a restart) and a live overlay (agent · foreground · live-PR · the PTY/xterm/attach handles — “never persisted; a restore must re-derive it”). That partition is active-vs-sleeping:

// surface.ts bases UNCHANGED — the fold already exists; exactly one field is added.
const ActiveTerminalSchema =
  PersistedTerminalFieldsSchema.merge(LiveTerminalFieldsSchema)   // live overlay present
    .extend({ state: z.literal("active") });

const SleepingTerminalSchema =
  PersistedTerminalFieldsSchema                                   // base only — overlay absent by type
    .extend({ state: z.literal("sleeping"), sleptAt: z.number() });

const TerminalMetadataSchema =                                   // the wire/collection shape
  z.discriminatedUnion("state", [ActiveTerminalSchema, SleepingTerminalSchema]);

type Terminal =
  | ({ state: "active" }                    & PersistedTerminalFields & LiveTerminalFields)
  | ({ state: "sleeping"; sleptAt: number } & PersistedTerminalFields);

// Reading: presence reads the union; touching a live field MUST narrow.
const placeTile  = (t: Terminal) => t.canvasLayout;             // both arms — no narrow
const routeInput = (t: Terminal) => {
  if (t.state !== "active") return;                             // compiler-forced narrow
  send(t /* now carries pr · agent · foreground + a live PTY */);
};

sleptAt is the sleeping arm’s analogue of the live overlay — the only new scalar. An active terminal is base + overlay; a sleeping terminal is base + sleptAt. Sleeping is one record whose state flag says whether its PTY is currently spawned.

Before → after

The registry’s whole stated job was to keep tile presence (exists, sits on the canvas, focusable, has a dock row) apart from terminal liveness (PTY · xterm · agent · attach). But it does that by indirection over a fold the schema already owns: tileIds is a verbatim re-export of terminalIds(), the selection signals are verbatim re-exports of view-state, contentOf fabricates a {kind:"terminal",terminalId:id} object on every read, and getLayout/setLayout are a one-kind dispatch. Move the discriminant onto the terminal and the layer has nothing left to do: presence consumers read the union, liveness consumers narrow, and there is no second store, no synthesized content object, and no separate dormant cell to merge.

The surgical collapse

It isn’t a git revert — the genuinely-good canvas primitives stay; only the presence-vs-liveness bridge goes. Tellingly, the two keystones #1435 advertised don’t even live in tile/: the reference-stability memo is in useTerminalMetadata.ts, the selection signals in useViewState.ts. So the entire tile/ directory is drop, and every keep either already lives outside it or only needs its import edge re-pointed from tileStore.* to store.*.

KeepWhy it stays
CanvasTile.tsx — the content-agnostic card shell (drag/resize, title bar, pan/zoom transform, mode union, aura/on-screen gating)A real UI primitive keyed on id: string + render-prop slots; knows nothing of terminals. The #988 wrapper-less transform is load-bearing canvas mechanics the sum never touches.
TerminalCanvas.tsx spatial machinery — pendingLayouts, placement cascade, drag/resize commit + grid-snap, focus/center effects, minimap dragPure geometry over tileIds: string[] + a layout seam; survives unchanged, only the seam re-points at the terminal store.
sameTerminalIdOrder + the terminalIds() memo (#1425 keystone)Already lives in useTerminalMetadata.ts; the re-export is what’s deleted. The metadata-only-tick equals gate keeps suppressing canvas/dock/mode churn.
persistCanvasLayout.ts; useCanvasArrange.ts domain logicDependency-light leaves / genuine canvas behavior. Algorithms untouched; plumbing re-points off tileStore.*.
DropReplaced by
tile/useTileStore.ts — the registry singletonThe terminal store itself, keyed by the Terminal union. Presence-vs-liveness is a state === "active" narrow, not a second store.
tile/tileContent.ts — TileContent peer union + TileId = TerminalId aliasThe discriminant on the terminal (state). No separate id space — terminal ids are canvas ids; the kind:"terminal" arm carried no information.
synthesize-on-read contentOf + the single-arm <Switch> dispatch<For> renders each terminal; the body narrows on terminal.state — active mounts the live xterm body, sleeping renders the frozen body off the base. No object fabricated per read.
getLayout/setLayout as “hide where layout lives” seamscanvasLayout is a base field on both arms — nothing to hide. Reads hit getMetadata(id)?.canvasLayout; writes hit persistCanvasLayout directly (gated on active). The fail-fast absent-record guard moves onto that path.
Re-exposed activeId/activate/setActiveSilently/tileCountRead view-state directly; tileCount() becomes terminalIds().length (counting sleeping). The wrong-store bug class disappears at the type level, so the re-export buys nothing.

The phases

Each is one reviewable PR; each leaves master shippable.

PhaseWhat landsWhy separable
1 — Collapse + seat the sumDelete tile/; re-point every consumer (canvas <For>, dock, minimap, arrange, view-posture, commands, right-panel) at the terminal store; add the state discriminant but ship with only the active arm populatedZero behavior change (every terminal is active) — a pure structural move that makes the narrowing seam exist and compile before any sleep logic does
2 — Sleep / Wake corePopulate the sleeping arm: sleep = snapshot the base + release PTY/xterm/agent (reuse kill-with-subs); wake = respawn from the base (reuse restore). Sleeping terminals join the one set the canvas/dock iterate — focusable/draggable tiles, selectable rows — with a dormant placeholder body and one Wake verb. Rehydrate-as-sleeping on bootThe user-facing core; the dedup-window and cross-arm-transition awkwardness are gone by construction
3 — Frozen screenshot bodyCapture via the existing screenshotTerminal path just before sleep, write under KOLU_STATE_DIR, serve through a small static image route (serve-dir / iframe-preview precedent); the reference rides the recordIsolated surface — one capture + one route; swaps Phase 2’s placeholder for a real frame
4 — Unify with session-restoreCollapse boot-restore and wake onto one seam: “rehydrate a persisted terminal by respawning its PTY from the base.” Boot = bulk wake, wake = activate-one, sleep = the inverse; the disjoint sleeping store and the session snapshot merge into one ordered terminal setBehavior-preserving consolidation, landed last, designed against three concrete callers instead of two hypothetical ones

Why the order is the point

Sleep was never the hard part — the machinery shipped in a day. The hard part was where a dormant terminal lives. Phase 1 stops answering that with a layer and answers it with a flag. Everything after is a body, a route, or a consolidation — small, because the structure finally fits the thing.

Dropped from earlier plans: the registry as a durable layer ( #1435 , collapsed in the new P1); the planned third variant (non-terminal content isn’t near — speculative generality); the “sleeping as a TileContent variant” framing ( #1441 , discarded); and the old P4 louder-alerts/switcher-parity grab-bag (polish — its slot now spends on the restore unification).

Payoff & trade-offs

Two wins fall out of putting the discriminant on the terminal:

  • Type-safe presence vs liveness. A consumer that touches pr/agent/foreground/PTY must first narrow state === "active" — the compiler refuses a live field on the bare union. The “wrong store” bug class ( #1441 ‘s minimap/mobile/cycle/arrange reading a live-only list) becomes unrepresentable, because there is no live-only list. The same narrow relocates focusedId() (the split-focus divergence is real and stays, but input-routing resolves inside the active narrow) and re-points holdsWebgl/webglTileBudget at the active set — a sleeping tile sits in the MRU and on the canvas yet holds no WebGL context, by construction.
  • Session-restore unification. SavedTerminal is already PersistedTerminalFields + id — the sleeping arm’s exact payload minus sleptAt. A boot-restored terminal and a slept terminal become the same on-disk record, distinguished only by state. One persistence channel; restore = bulk wake, wake = restore-one, sleep = the inverse.

And the costs, named so they’re chosen, not discovered:

  • A migration is mandatory. SavedTerminalSchema is persisted, so adding state needs a state.ts migration defaulting legacy records to "active" + a SCHEMA_VERSION bump — the discriminatedUnion rejects a record with no state until the migration stamps it. That is the one sanctioned place to supply the legacy default; read sites must narrow, never coalesce (no read-time ?? "active").
  • A wide type-level ripple is the diff. Flipping TerminalMetadataSchema from a flat .merge to a discriminatedUnion forces every liveness reader to either stay in the presence subset or add a narrow. That sweep is the win, but it is the bulk of the work.
  • state must be lifecycle-owned. Only the sleep/wake transitions write state; a metadata provider must never resurrect a live field on a sleeping record (the overlay isn’t on that arm — a type error, which is the point).

When we'd revisit

If a genuine non-terminal canvas kind ever lands — a sticky note, a web embed — reintroduce a content-kind layer, but designed against two real kinds, not the one-armed speculative union we’re removing now. Location is structure: the receptacle is justified the day there’s a second thing to put in it, and not before. (See electricity.mdx — the same trap, named three ways.)
